methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Prusa Research is a bootstrapped frontier-tech company operating in Robotics & Manufacturing, founded by Josef Průša. The valuation universe comprises 9 total comparable companies: 1 public entity (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. However, the narrow peer set presents significant methodological constraints.

The Peer-Median triangulation method is not applicable to this valuation. While 12 same-sector peers exist within Robotics & Manufacturing, filtering for matching stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single category. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors, manufacturing focus areas, and capital structures. No alternative triangulation methods are supported by the provided dataset.

The absence of sufficient stage-matched or tier-matched comparables prevents quantitative synthesis at this time. Any valuation range derived from this dataset would lack analytical rigor. Key uncertainties include: the capital intensity and pathway to profitability relative to the identified private peers; Prusa Research's revenue scale and unit economics relative to public and private benchmarks; and whether the broader 12-company Robotics & Manufacturing universe contains meaningful structural parallels. A credible valuation would require either expanded peer filtering (e.g., by subsector or business model) or alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis, for which no input data is provided.
